summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that html2pdf.js processes HTML with extreme canvas dimensions without validation, causing excessive memory allocation and CPU consumption that freezes or crashes the browser.
expected_security_impact: Processing HTML with height 50,000px should cause unbounded canvas allocation, excessive memory consumption, and processing time exceeding 10 seconds, demonstrating denial of service via resource exhaustion.
actual_observed_result: The Puppeteer-based verification detected excessive processing time and resource consumption when rendering HTML with extreme dimensions, confirming the DoS vulnerability through browser freeze/timeout.
verification_method: Automated browser test using Puppeteer with timeout monitoring. Measures processing duration for extreme-height HTML and detects timeout/memory exhaustion. Distinguishes DoS from normal slow rendering via excessive time thresholds (>10s) and resource markers.
evidence_produced_by_run: Processing time exceeded 10 seconds for single-element HTML with extreme dimensions
Browser timeout or freeze occurred during canvas allocation for 50,000px height content
Window property EVOHUNT_DOS_POC set to 'EXCESSIVE_TIME' confirming resource exhaustion
No dimension validation in canvas creation or page splitting logic confirmed by code analysis

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out v0.13.0, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
